items.title: Nina Dobrev Takes the Bridal Trend Beyond White Satin in a Sheer, Feathered Two-piece at Taormina
items.summary: The actress paired a made-to-order floral-jacquard corset with a crystal-embroidered tulle skirt.

items.title: Perfume brand Heretic is leaning into the 'B-side' of pop culture, one unexpected collab at a time
items.summary: Indie-perfume brand Heretic uses unexpected collaborations with music, art and pop culture to drive DTC growth and brand loyalty in the US fragrance market.

items.title: Luxury Briefing: Mytheresa is using AI to find future VIPs
items.summary: Mytheresa leverages AI to identify and nurture future high-value customers, signaling AI adoption in luxury beauty retail.

items.title: Glossy Podcast: Quince head of brand strategy on how the brand is capitalizing on its $10B valuation
items.summary: Direct-from-manufacturer Quince ($10B valuation) is expanding into beauty and skincare, bypassing traditional retail.

items.title: Where to invest, sell and set boundaries: The 6 truths guiding brands' next steps
items.summary: At Glossy's E-Commerce Summit in Miami, executives outlined 6 strategic truths for beauty brands: DTC resilience, TikTok Shop expansion, and omnichannel investment.

items.title: Why the World Cup is a make or break moment for retail this summer
items.summary: Brands including beauty retailers are activating around the 2026 FIFA World Cup in North America as a major cultural commerce moment.

items.title: Saie bets on education with 'The Makeup Class' to create brand 'superfans'
items.summary: Clean beauty brand Saie launched 'The Makeup Class' IRL masterclass program in US cities to turn customers into brand superfans and drive DTC loyalty.

items.title: Open-box marketplace Rebel is now selling better-for-you snacks
items.summary: Open-box marketplace Rebel ($25M Series B) expands beyond electronics into wellness and beauty adjacent categories.

items.title: Dr. Muneeb Shah's Remedy Raises $20M Series A Round As Investors Double Down On Clinical Skincare
items.summary: Dr. Muneeb Shah's clinical skincare brand Remedy closes $20M Series A (L Catterton + Norwest). Investors are doubling down on doctor-founded, evidence-based mass-market skincare brands targeting US consumers.

items.title: Capsum Acquires KKT Labs And Taps Krupa Koestline To Lead U.S. Operations
items.summary: French beauty tech/biotech company Capsum acquires US-based KKT Labs and appoints Krupa Koestline as US operations lead, signaling expansion of advanced formulation tech into North American market.

items.title: Scenes from Walmart's annual Associates Week and shareholder meeting
items.summary: Walmart emphasizes trendiest assortment strategy, signaling continued beauty category expansion as it competes with Target and Amazon.

items.title: Marketplace Briefing: Online merchants welcome Trump customs crackdown amid wave of tariff evasion pitches
items.summary: Tariff crackdown on online merchants: de minimis loophole tightening affects cross-border beauty sellers on Amazon and Temu/Shein, raising compliance costs for Chinese sellers.

items.title: Derm-Founded More Than Nine Wants To End Skincare Decision Fatigue For Moms
items.summary: Derm-founded brand More Than Nine launches simplified skincare for pregnant/new mothers, addressing the growing maternal skincare segment in the US.

items.title: Bubble Founder Shai Eisenman On Winning Across Generations, Gen Alpha And Not Selling (For Now)
items.summary: Bubble skincare brand (available at CVS, Target, Walmart) is winning Gen Alpha and Gen Z customers across mass retail, with founder refusing acquisition offers. Gen Alpha skincare segment is booming in North American mass market.

items.title: How meat sticks players are trying to find white space
items.summary: Adjacent wellness category growth; brands finding white space in adjacent lifestyle categories relevant to beauty cross-sell.

items.title: Dr. Muneeb Shah's Remedy: Clinical Skincare $20M Series A - Evidence-Based Mass Market Skincare is Hot
items.summary: Dr. Muneeb Shah (social media dermatologist) brand Remedy raises $20M from L Catterton and Norwest Venture Partners. Clinical/doctor-founded skincare brands addressing everyday skin concerns at mass-market price points are attracting major VC investment. Key message: evidence-based, dermatologist-backed, accessible price, targeting everyday US consumer. Keywords: clinical skincare, dermatologist, mass skincare, L Catterton, North America.

items.title: Bubble Skincare Gen Alpha Win: Mass-Market Youth Skincare Dominates CVS/Target/Walmart
items.summary: Bubble skincare brand winning Gen Alpha and Gen Z at CVS, Target, Walmart. Founder Shai Eisenman cites 'skincare fatigue' among younger consumers craving simplicity and clean ingredients. Not selling despite M&A interest - brand valued highly. Hot trend: affordable, clean, simple skincare for ages 8-24 at mass-market retailers. Price point $10-15. North American mass beauty boom.

items.title: Amazon Beauty Bestsellers TOP10: Korean Skincare, Acne Patches, Exfoliating Toners Dominate
items.summary: Amazon Beauty & Personal Care TOP10 (June 2026): #1 eos Shea Body Lotion ($9.97, 75K reviews), #2 medicube Korean Toner Pads ($18.90, 25K reviews), #4 Mighty Patch Acne Patches ($12.99, 184K reviews), #5 The Ordinary Glycolic Acid Toner ($13.50, 53K reviews), #6 Neutrogena Makeup Remover Wipes ($9.97, 119K reviews), #8 BIODANCE Bio-Collagen Mask ($19, 45K reviews). Trends: Korean skincare, acne care, affordable exfoliation, body care.
